jokes.text: As long as nothing happens for the next 15 minutes, our insurance will be reinstated. So, let's just sit quietly and run out the clock.
jokes.setup: Character announces need for nothing to happen to maintain insurance
jokes.punchline: The very act of announcing this virtually guarantees chaos will ensue
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for disaster - the bureaucratic jinx combined with sitcom inevitability. Well-structured ironic setup.

jokes.text: A giant sausage!
jokes.setup: Hermes just said nothing should happen
jokes.punchline: Immediate chaos with absurd threat identification
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy. The immediate subversion of Hermes' hope with maximum absurdity in minimal words.

jokes.text: Oh, God, no! Get out of here, you horrific sausage! Wait. Isn't today the parade? It's just a giant balloon!
jokes.setup: Hermes panics about horrific sausage threat
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's harmless parade balloon, deflating his own terror
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection payoff, 'horrific sausage' is quotable, but resolution is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: See, buddy, it's not so scary. I feel better now.
jokes.setup: Hermes comforting Fry about the balloon
jokes.punchline: Fry's deadpan response suggests he's the one being comforted despite being braver
jokes.explanation: Mild role reversal joke, functional but not particularly memorable or impactful.

jokes.text: Why are so many different parades smooshed together? It's the Parade Day Parade. What's Parade Day?
jokes.setup: Fry notices parade complexity
jokes.punchline: The recursive naming 'Parade Day Parade' for a day dedicated to parades
jokes.explanation: Good futuristic worldbuilding humor with recursive naming convention that feels authentically bureaucratic.

jokes.text: Why, when I was a boy, we had a parade every day. Those were dark times.
jokes.setup: Professor reminisces about daily parades
jokes.punchline: Characterizes constant celebration as 'dark times'
jokes.explanation: Excellent Professor-specific delivery. Perfect subversion of nostalgia with absurd complaint about too much celebration. Classic Futurama deadpan.

jokes.text: Hey, look, a fog is rolling in. No, that's just the Jamaican Pride float.
jokes.setup: Amy misidentifies visual phenomenon as weather
jokes.punchline: It's actually smoke/steam from stereotype-based float
jokes.explanation: Relies on stereotype for humor, somewhat cheap but executed with good timing and misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Oh, no! It's within munching distance of the Doritos float!
jokes.setup: Parade float collision danger
jokes.punchline: 'Munching distance' as a unit of measurement for snack-related disasters
jokes.explanation: Creative terminology that's both absurd and logical within context. Good escalation of parade disaster.

jokes.text: Kif, you're slacking off on my waving arm.
jokes.setup: Zapp is in parade, presumably waving to crowds
jokes.punchline: He's making Kif operate his waving arm rather than doing it himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp Brannigan character moment - peak narcissistic laziness. Only he would outsource his own parade waving.

jokes.text: Whoa. Sweet shirt, dude. Thanks. I have another one that says 'Paleontologists Do It In The Dirt.' I'm a paleontologist.
jokes.setup: Compliment on shirt leads to reveal of second shirt
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about innuendo shirt and profession in danger moment
jokes.explanation: Standard nerd oversharing trope, somewhat predictable but character-appropriate timing during crisis.

jokes.text: Oh, honey, I dig you so much.
jokes.setup: Paleontologist husband just revealed his profession
jokes.punchline: Wife uses dig pun in romantic context during disaster
jokes.explanation: Obvious pun that's mildly cringeworthy by design, but lacks originality or surprise.

jokes.text: This one's for the deadbolt, this is for the top lock. I think this one's for the knob. The city's in a bad neighborhood.
jokes.setup: Ceremonial presentation of city keys
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're actual functional keys because city is in bad area
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of ceremonial tradition. Takes metaphorical honor and makes it literal necessity. Great escalating detail.

jokes.text: Heroes don't do drugs. Except for Drugman, I guess.
jokes.setup: Fry delivers moral platitude about heroes
jokes.punchline: Immediately undermines it with exception for hypothetical drug-themed hero
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - tries to be profound but immediately finds the loophole. Great character-specific humor with quotable delivery.

jokes.text: It sure was nice of the mayor's wife to have sex with me. Uh?
jokes.setup: Fry reflecting on his heroic day
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions sexual encounter as if it's normal hero reward
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Fry's characteristic obliviousness to social appropriateness. Escalates the hero treatment absurdly.

jokes.text: You could have saved a robot, but instead, you saved a human. Do you think human life is more precious than robot life?
jokes.setup: Bender confronts Fry about his rescue choice
jokes.punchline: Forces uncomfortable moral question about species hierarchy
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender jealousy disguised as philosophical inquiry. Sets up central conflict of episode with character-specific logic.

jokes.text: Bender, you always say you're going to kill yourself, but you almost never do.
jokes.setup: Bender threatens suicide again
jokes.punchline: 'Almost never do' suggests he occasionally follows through
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement that implies Bender has successfully committed suicide before. Perfect character consistency acknowledgment.

jokes.text: Yeah. Last time you went to the suicide booth, you ended up dating it for six months. How is Lynn, by the way?
jokes.setup: References Bender's past suicide attempt
jokes.punchline: Reveals he formed romantic relationship with suicide machine
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that adds new absurd layer to established lore. Perfect Bender behavior - even suicide becomes romantic comedy.

jokes.text: Living in Oregon with her crazy mother.
jokes.setup: Leela asks about Bender's suicide booth girlfriend
jokes.punchline: Bender treats machine relationship like normal breakup with family drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect continuation of absurd premise. Bender's deadpan delivery of machine family drama as if completely normal.

jokes.text: Fat, but great. I thought you were in Oregon. My mom tried to commit suicide in me. I don't want to talk about it.
jokes.setup: Bender reunites with suicide booth girlfriend Lynn
jokes.punchline: Lynn reveals disturbing family dynamic involving suicidal parent machine
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark machine family psychology. 'Suicide in me' creates disturbing but funny image of machine dysfunction.

jokes.text: Oh, no. You're going to die, all right, but it won't be suicide. It'll be murder.
jokes.setup: Bender wants to commit suicide via Lynn
jokes.punchline: Lynn threatens murder instead, escalating the violence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of machine relationship drama. Turns suicide request into murder threat with appropriate menace.

jokes.text: No way, mon. He's faking it.
jokes.setup: Bender appears to have been killed
jokes.punchline: Hermes immediately suspects deception due to Bender's history
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - Hermes knows Bender well enough to doubt his death. Sets up misdirection.

jokes.text: Our what? It's a sign of respect, you savage!
jokes.setup: Hermes mentions 'taking-off caps'
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg doesn't know funeral etiquette, gets insulted as savage
jokes.explanation: Typical Zoidberg cultural confusion with Hermes' bureaucratic indignation. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: He may be gone, but he'll always be with us as a trash can.
jokes.setup: Leela tries to comfort mourning friends
jokes.punchline: Reduces Bender's legacy to his most basic function
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny comfort attempt that's accidentally insulting. Great deadpan delivery reducing friend to appliance function.

jokes.text: Look at that loser getting garbage dumped in him. Glad I'm not him, whoever he is.
jokes.setup: Bender looking at his own body being used as trash can
jokes.punchline: Doesn't recognize his own body and insults himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Bender's vanity prevents him from recognizing his own degraded state, leading to self-insult

jokes.text: Just don't pick his nose! Ha, ha, ha, ha. What's wrong with you guys? This is comedy gold.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg makes pun about picking Bender's body parts
jokes.punchline: Desperately defends his bad joke as 'comedy gold' when nobody laughs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - making inappropriate joke then doubling down when it fails. Meta-commentary on bad comedy timing.

jokes.text: He always had my back. So I'll take his arm. As a back-scratcher.
jokes.setup: Fry sentimentally remembers Bender having his back
jokes.punchline: Wants to literally use Bender's arm as back-scratcher
jokes.explanation: Dark but sweet sentiment turned practical. Good wordplay connecting emotional metaphor to literal function.

jokes.text: Hey, dip-schlitz, that's not me. I'm right here. Hello? Pay attention to me!
jokes.setup: Bender realizes friends can't hear him as ghost
jokes.punchline: Desperately tries to get attention with increasing frustration and insults
jokes.explanation: Good ghost frustration comedy. 'Dip-schlitz' is creative insult. Shows Bender's need for attention even in death.

jokes.text: Oh, you're no fun. You see, Bender, it's simple. You're a ghost. A ghost? No, just the regular kind.
jokes.setup: Robot Devil explains Bender's condition
jokes.punchline: Dismisses 'special' ghost status - he's just regular dead
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan deflation of Bender's potential specialness. Robot Devil's casual dismissal works well.

jokes.text: Adoy. Wait. Adoy?
jokes.setup: Robot Devil explains Bender's ghost status
jokes.punchline: Bender uses his catchphrase then questions it
jokes.explanation: Mild self-aware moment with Bender questioning his own speech pattern

jokes.text: An infinite loop? I don't have time for that!
jokes.setup: Robot Devil explains Bender is stuck in infinite loop
jokes.punchline: Bender worried about time constraints in eternity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - worried about scheduling in infinity. Great irony of time concern in timeless state.

jokes.text: Fry? I hate that guy. We once traded hands. They still stink of candy corn.
jokes.setup: Robot Devil mentions knowing Fry
jokes.punchline: Reveals bizarre past interaction with lingering sensory consequence
jokes.explanation: Good callback to previous episode. Candy corn detail adds specific gross-out humor to abstract concept.

jokes.text: I'm not stupid. So, yes, absolutely. What have I agreed to?
jokes.setup: Robot Devil asks if Bender wants to make deal
jokes.punchline: Bender claims he's not stupid while immediately proving he is
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Bender's overconfidence and impulsiveness. Great self-contradicting dialogue.

jokes.text: Which, as luck would have it, is where I rehearse my band. Hit it, boys! Cigars are evil, you won't miss 'em... Okay, I'll do it, I'll do it! Just stop the damn music!
jokes.setup: Robot Devil threatens Bender with robot hell
jokes.punchline: Hell's torture is bad musical rehearsal, immediately breaks Bender
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Robot Devil's musical nature. Hell as bad rehearsal space is creative torture concept.

jokes.text: Hmm. Soap. I know Fry's afraid of that. Maybe he'll die if I rub him all over with it, real slow.
jokes.setup: Bender tries to scare Fry with soap
jokes.punchline: Plan sounds more sensual than scary due to phrasing
jokes.explanation: Bender's obliviousness to innuendo in his ghost plan. Character-appropriate but relatively mild humor.

jokes.text: No! Not the armpits! Winter's coming!
jokes.setup: Fry being attacked by automated bathroom
jokes.punchline: Specific fear about armpit grooming with seasonal reasoning
jokes.explanation: Absurd specific fear with practical seasonal logic. Good physical comedy setup but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Bite my shiny metal ass. Help! I was attacked in my bathroom! By my bathroom!
jokes.setup: Zoidberg delivers Bender's catchphrase, Fry reports attack
jokes.punchline: Bathroom itself as attacker creates absurd threat
jokes.explanation: Good use of Bender's catchphrase by wrong character. 'By my bathroom' is good escalation of threat source.

jokes.text: Hold onto your dookie. It's about to get spooky!
jokes.setup: Bender preparing to haunt Fry more
jokes.punchline: Crude rhyming threat with bathroom humor
jokes.explanation: Simple rhyming threat with scatological humor. Character-appropriate but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Who you gonna call? Gho... The number you have dialed has been lame since 1989.
jokes.setup: Fry starts classic Ghostbusters reference
jokes.punchline: Phone system insults the outdated reference
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on overused pop culture references. Phone system as cultural critic is creative.

jokes.text: I said science! Let the seance begin.
jokes.setup: Professor insists on scientific explanation
jokes.punchline: Amy immediately starts unscientific seance
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction between Professor's scientific stance and Amy's mystical approach. Efficient setup/payoff.

jokes.text: You said something about a buffet? I'm right here, you lazy shyster.
jokes.setup: Psychic claimed to sense spirits
jokes.punchline: Professor only interested in food, Bender frustrated at being ignored
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Professor's priorities and Bender's continued invisibility frustration.

jokes.text: There's no such thing as ghosts, you donkey-monkey. It's a robot ghost.
jokes.setup: Professor denies ghosts exist
jokes.punchline: Immediately specifies it's robot ghost, contradicting himself
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment of self-contradiction. 'Donkey-monkey' is creative insult compound.

jokes.text: Oh, lord. I got the too-much-macaroni sweats.
jokes.setup: Scruffy at buffet
jokes.punchline: Specific medical condition from pasta overconsumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy line - oddly specific condition delivered with characteristic stoicism. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: It also keeps cats off the sofa by killing 'em.
jokes.setup: Preacher gives Fry protective charm
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions lethal side effect as bonus feature
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor delivered casually. Escalates blessing into death threat with perfect deadpan delivery.

jokes.text: Survived a heart attack? Damn you, Obama-care!
jokes.setup: Robot Devil checks if Fry died
jokes.punchline: Blames healthcare system for keeping victim alive
jokes.explanation: Topical political reference that inverts typical complaint about healthcare. Moderately funny but relies on real-world reference.

jokes.text: Now that he's gone, I realize how valuable a robot life can be, when it belongs to my best friend.
jokes.setup: Fry reflects on Bender's death
jokes.punchline: Learns lesson about robot life value through loss
jokes.explanation: Important character growth moment. Addresses episode's central moral question with genuine emotion.

jokes.text: As a reminder, passengers are limited to two carry-on butter churns.
jokes.setup: Flight to Amish planet boarding announcement
jokes.punchline: Airline restrictions adapted for Amish passengers
jokes.explanation: Good world-building humor - logical extension of airline bureaucracy to unusual passenger needs

jokes.text: Well, yank my beard. This be a surprise.
jokes.setup: Fry has been living with Amish
jokes.punchline: Adopted archaic speech patterns and exclamations
jokes.explanation: Good character adaptation showing Fry's linguistic absorption. 'Yank my beard' is creative archaic exclamation.

jokes.text: She's naked but unharmed.
jokes.setup: Asked what happened to Granny Hester
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific reassurance that raises more questions
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd reassurance that's simultaneously comforting and disturbing. Perfect economy of weirdness.

jokes.text: Of course not. What idiot thinks that? You. You're using my own words against me. Go to hell!
jokes.setup: Robot Devil asks if robot life is worth more
jokes.punchline: Bender realizes hypocrisy and gets angry at manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment where Bender confronts his own prejudice. Well-structured realization and anger.

jokes.text: I'm afraid your friend is about to be fully buckminstered!
jokes.setup: Fry in danger from charging animals
jokes.punchline: Buckminster Fuller reference as verb for geometric destruction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant architectural reference turned into death threat. Very creative vocabulary creation that's highly quotable.

jokes.text: There's one, you rusty old dummy.
jokes.setup: Robot Devil claiming no machines on Amish planet
jokes.punchline: Bender realizes Robot Devil himself is a machine
jokes.explanation: Good plot resolution through character logic - Bender's insult delivery makes the revelation more satisfying

jokes.text: Well, I think the Robot Devil said he loved me in Bender's voice wearing Granny Hester's clothes.
jokes.setup: Friends ask what happened
jokes.punchline: Fry's confused summary of supernatural cross-dressing love declaration
jokes.explanation: Excellent confused summary that captures absurdity of supernatural events. Great escalation of confusion.

jokes.text: Shut up, God! Beg pardon? I want to go back to Robot Earth. I mean regular Earth.
jokes.setup: God welcomes Bender to Robot Heaven
jokes.punchline: Bender rudely rejects paradise, then awkwardly apologizes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - tells God to shut up then immediately realizes social error. Peak character-specific irreverence with perfect comedic timing.

jokes.text: Hey, wait, did you haunt me?
jokes.setup: Fry happy to see Bender alive
jokes.punchline: Delayed realization about the ghostly harassment
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Fry's delayed processing - emotional reunion followed by logical question
